w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: To verify that exactly 25.0 minutes of continuous excursion (6 consecutive data points) is strictly identified as a violation.
Create a test CSV (`1-P001_04-23-26_00-00.csv`) with 6 consecutive rows at 5-minute intervals (12:00 to 12:25).
Launch `AirQualityReview.exe`, select the test folder, and click "Analyze".
The report explicitly flags the status as "Out of spec".
To verify that excursions of 20 minutes (5 points) are correctly ignored as "Passed".
Create a test CSV with only 5 consecutive out-of-spec points (12:00 to 12:20).
Process the file through the Analysis engine.
The report status for Temperature is "Passed".
OQ-TC-03: Data Gap Hardening (10-min Continuity Rule)
To verify that a gap of exactly 10 minutes (600s) joins blocks, but a gap > 600s resets the counter.
Test A: Create a CSV with two 15-min blocks separated by a 10-minute gap.
Test B: Create a CSV with two 15-min blocks separated by a 10 min 1 sec gap.
Test A: System joins the blocks into one continuous violation.
System resets the counter; no violation is triggered (each block < 25 mins).
OQ-TC-04: Pressure Comparison - Exact Timestamp Matching (Inner Join)
To verify that the system calculates pressure deltas only when timestamps match exactly.
Prepare Room File with a record at `12:00:00`.
Prepare Corridor File with a record at `12:00:00` and another at `12:05:01`.
Run the analysis for the Room.
The `12:05:01` record is ignored (Inner Join functionality verified).
OQ-TC-05: Audit Trail Hash-Chain Tamper Detection
To verify the system detects hash-chain manipulation.
Modify a single character in an `entry_hash`. Save and close.
GUI displays "FATAL ERROR 004: Audit Trail Integrity Check Failed".
To ensure redundant data rows with identical timestamps do not double-count duration.
Insert a duplicate row (same timestamp, same value) into a source CSV.
The system retains only the first occurrence.
The total violation duration calculation remains accurate.
To verify clear error messages for common failure modes.
Process a CSV with no `<>Date` column.
Attempt to analyze a directory with restricted read permissions.
GUI modals contain Error Icons and "OK" buttons.
To verify summary statistics are mathematically accurate.
Process a dataset with 3 known values (e.g., 20.0, 21.0, 22.0).
Compare with the console log and report.
Report values perfectly match manual calculations.
To ensure the validated version is consistently displayed.
View Cell A2 in the Excel Report.
To verify every processed raw data file is uniquely identified.
Perform an analysis on 3 CSV files.
Each entry contains a valid SHA-256 hash of the respective CSV file.
To verify adherence to corporate document standards.
Date cells are merged across columns A to D with gray backgrounds.
To ensure the limits used for analysis are traceable.
The log entry includes the `Limit_Hash` field containing the SHA-256 of `SetPointLimit.xlsx`.
To verify stability when encountering non-numeric values.
Edit a CSV, changing a numeric value to `"ERROR_DATA"`.
The invalid data is treated as NaN and gracefully ignored.
To verify the system prevents analysis if specifications are missing.
OQ-TC-15: UI Date Range Filtering Accuracy
To verify the system slices data correctly based on user selection.
Use a folder with data spanning Jan 1st to Jan 31st.
Select range Jan 5th to Jan 10th in the GUI.
The generated report only contains headers and records for Jan 5 to Jan 10.
To verify the system's ability to split a bulk Module 5 CSV.
Navigate to 'Data Transformation' in the UI.
Select `Mo.5(T-H-P).csv` and set an Output Directory.
OQ-TC-17: Data Transformation - Main Plant Splitting
To verify the system consolidates RMT, RMH, RPT files.
Select the three BAS files (RMT, RMH, RPT) in the Transformation UI.
Set an Output Directory and click **'Split Main Plant Report'**.
Generates unified room CSVs containing T, H, and P.
Point descriptions in headers match the dynamic source mappings.
